pdg_node.statement: (sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> tuple[positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), float scalar]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Convert all clinically-meaningful time windows (50 ms, 100 ms, 200 ms, 300 ms, 350 ms, 1200 ms) from seconds into integer sample counts given the sampling rate, and fix the scalar threshold M_th.

declaration.name: compute_timing_constants
declaration.type_signature: (sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> tuple[positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), positive int (samples), float scalar]

pdg_node.statement: (signal: 1-D float array (ECG samples), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as signal
pdg_node.informal_desc: Apply two successive moving-average FIR filters (zero-phase via filtfilt): a 20 ms boxcar to suppress 50 Hz power-line interference and a ~28 ms boxcar to suppress EMG noise above ~35 Hz.

declaration.name: suppress_powerline_and_emg_noise
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array (ECG samples), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as signal
declaration.docstring: Apply two successive moving-average FIR filters (zero-phase via filtfilt): a 20 ms boxcar to suppress 50 Hz power-line interference and a ~28 ms boxcar to suppress EMG noise above ~35 Hz.

declaration.name: suppress_powerline_and_emg_noise
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array (ECG samples), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as signal
declaration.docstring: Apply two successive moving-average FIR filters (zero-phase via filtfilt): a 20 ms boxcar to suppress 50 Hz power-line interference and a ~28 ms boxcar to suppress EMG noise above ~35 Hz.

declaration.name: suppress_powerline_and_emg_noise
declaration.type_signature: (signal: 1-D float array (ECG samples), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as signal
declaration.docstring: Apply two successive moving-average FIR filters (zero-phase via filtfilt): a 20 ms boxcar to suppress 50 Hz power-line interference and a ~28 ms boxcar to suppress EMG noise above ~35 Hz.

pdg_node.statement: (X_ma: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as X_ma
pdg_node.informal_desc: Apply a 7th-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 40 Hz followed by a 7th-order Butterworth high-pass filter at 9 Hz, yielding a band-limited signal retaining the QRS frequency band.

declaration.name: bandpass_filter_signal
declaration.type_signature: (X_ma: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as X_ma
declaration.docstring: Apply a 7th-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 40 Hz followed by a 7th-order Butterworth high-pass filter at 9 Hz, yielding a band-limited signal retaining the QRS frequency band.

declaration.name: bandpass_filter_signal
declaration.type_signature: (X_ma: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as X_ma
declaration.docstring: Apply a 7th-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 40 Hz followed by a 7th-order Butterworth high-pass filter at 9 Hz, yielding a band-limited signal retaining the QRS frequency band.

declaration.name: bandpass_filter_signal
declaration.type_signature: (X_ma: 1-D float array, sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as X_ma
declaration.docstring: Apply a 7th-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 40 Hz followed by a 7th-order Butterworth high-pass filter at 9 Hz, yielding a band-limited signal retaining the QRS frequency band.

pdg_node.statement: (X_bp: 1-D float array (band-passed ECG)) -> 1-D float array, length = len(X_bp)-2
pdg_node.informal_desc: Apply the Teager-Kaiser nonlinear energy operator Y[n] = X[n]^2 - X[n-1]*X[n+1] across the band-passed signal, then clip negative values to zero to produce a non-negative instantaneous energy envelope.

declaration.name: compute_nonlinear_energy_operator
declaration.type_signature: (X_bp: 1-D float array (band-passed ECG)) -> 1-D float array, length = len(X_bp)-2
declaration.docstring: Apply the Teager-Kaiser nonlinear energy operator Y[n] = X[n]^2 - X[n-1]*X[n+1] across the band-passed signal, then clip negative values to zero to produce a non-negative instantaneous energy envelope.

declaration.name: compute_nonlinear_energy_operator
declaration.type_signature: (X_bp: 1-D float array (band-passed ECG)) -> 1-D float array, length = len(X_bp)-2
declaration.docstring: Apply the Teager-Kaiser nonlinear energy operator Y[n] = X[n]^2 - X[n-1]*X[n+1] across the band-passed signal, then clip negative values to zero to produce a non-negative instantaneous energy envelope.

declaration.name: compute_nonlinear_energy_operator
declaration.type_signature: (X_bp: 1-D float array (band-passed ECG)) -> 1-D float array, length = len(X_bp)-2
declaration.docstring: Apply the Teager-Kaiser nonlinear energy operator Y[n] = X[n]^2 - X[n-1]*X[n+1] across the band-passed signal, then clip negative values to zero to produce a non-negative instantaneous energy envelope.

pdg_node.statement: (Y_nleo: 1-D float array (non-negative energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as Y_nleo
pdg_node.informal_desc: Apply a causal 40 ms moving-average FIR filter (first zero ~25 Hz) to the nonlinear energy signal to suppress differentiation-magnified noise.

declaration.name: smooth_energy_envelope
declaration.type_signature: (Y_nleo: 1-D float array (non-negative energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as Y_nleo
declaration.docstring: Apply a causal 40 ms moving-average FIR filter (first zero ~25 Hz) to the nonlinear energy signal to suppress differentiation-magnified noise.

declaration.name: smooth_energy_envelope
declaration.type_signature: (Y_nleo: 1-D float array (non-negative energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as Y_nleo
declaration.docstring: Apply a causal 40 ms moving-average FIR filter (first zero ~25 Hz) to the nonlinear energy signal to suppress differentiation-magnified noise.

declaration.name: smooth_energy_envelope
declaration.type_signature: (Y_nleo: 1-D float array (non-negative energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz)) -> 1-D float array, same length as Y_nleo
declaration.docstring: Apply a causal 40 ms moving-average FIR filter (first zero ~25 Hz) to the nonlinear energy signal to suppress differentiation-magnified noise.

pdg_node.statement: (Y: 1-D float array (smoothed energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz), M_th: float scalar, v350ms: positive int (samples)) -> tuple[1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (current threshold M), float scalar, 1-D float array, length=int(sampling_rate), float scalar, float scalar, 1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (mean RR), float scalar (baseline frequency threshold), list of int (detected QRS sample indices), list of int (R-peak sample indices)]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Bootstrap all adaptive-threshold state variables from the first 5 s of the smoothed energy: initialise the 5-slot MM buffer and its index, compute initial M (mean of MM), build the 1-s linear decay slope vector, zero-initialise R/RR/Rm/Rdec, set the baseline F from the first 350 ms of Y, and clear the QRS/Rpeak accumulators.

declaration.name: initialise_adaptive_state
declaration.type_signature: (Y: 1-D float array (smoothed energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz), M_th: float scalar, v350ms: positive int (samples)) -> tuple[1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (current threshold M), float scalar, 1-D float array, length=int(sampling_rate), float scalar, float scalar, 1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (mean RR), float scalar (baseline frequency threshold), list of int (detected QRS sample indices), list of int (R-peak sample indices)]
declaration.docstring: Bootstrap all adaptive-threshold state variables from the first 5 s of the smoothed energy: initialise the 5-slot MM buffer and its index, compute initial M (mean of MM), build the 1-s linear decay slope vector, zero-initialise R/RR/Rm/Rdec, set the baseline F from the first 350 ms of Y, and clear the QRS/Rpeak accumulators.

declaration.type_signature: (Y: 1-D float array (smoothed energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz), M_th: float scalar, v350ms: positive int (samples)) -> tuple[1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (current threshold M), float scalar, 1-D float array, length=int(sampling_rate), float scalar, float scalar, 1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (mean RR), float scalar (baseline frequency threshold), list of int (detected QRS sample indices), list of int (R-peak sample indices)]

declaration.name: initialise_adaptive_state
declaration.type_signature: (Y: 1-D float array (smoothed energy), sampling_rate: positive float (Hz), M_th: float scalar, v350ms: positive int (samples)) -> tuple[1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (current threshold M), float scalar, 1-D float array, length=int(sampling_rate), float scalar, float scalar, 1-D float array, length 5 (circular buffer), int in [0,4], float scalar (mean RR), float scalar (baseline frequency threshold), list of int (detected QRS sample indices), list of int (R-peak sample indices)]
declaration.docstring: Bootstrap all adaptive-threshold state variables from the first 5 s of the smoothed energy: initialise the 5-slot MM buffer and its index, compute initial M (mean of MM), build the 1-s linear decay slope vector, zero-initialise R/RR/Rm/Rdec, set the baseline F from the first 350 ms of Y, and clear the QRS/Rpeak accumulators.

pdg_node.statement: (Y: 1-D float array (smoothed energy), MM: 1-D float array, length 5, MMidx: int, M: float scalar, Mtemp: float scalar, slope: 1-D float array, R: float scalar, Rdec: float scalar, RR: 1-D float array, length 5, RRidx: int, Rm: float scalar, F: float scalar, QRS: list of int, Rpeak: list of int, M_th: float scalar, v50ms: positive int (samples), v200ms: positive int (samples), v300ms: positive int (samples), v350ms: positive int (samples), v1200ms: positive int (samples)) -> tuple[list of int (threshold-crossing sample indices), list of int (Y-max within 300 ms of each QRS)]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Iterate sample-by-sample through Y, maintaining the combined adaptive threshold MFR = M + F + R. After each QRS event: refresh MM and M within 200 ms, decay M from 200–1200 ms via the slope vector, hold/decay R around the expected RR interval. Detect a new QRS when Y[n] >= MFR (outside the 200 ms blanking period), record the local Y-maximum within 300 ms as the R-peak candidate, update the RR circular buffer, and update F each sample using the sliding 350 ms window rule.

declaration.name: run_adaptive_threshold_detection
declaration.type_signature: (Y: 1-D float array (smoothed energy), MM: 1-D float array, length 5, MMidx: int, M: float scalar, Mtemp: float scalar, slope: 1-D float array, R: float scalar, Rdec: float scalar, RR: 1-D float array, length 5, RRidx: int, Rm: float scalar, F: float scalar, QRS: list of int, Rpeak: list of int, M_th: float scalar, v50ms: positive int (samples), v200ms: positive int (samples), v300ms: positive int (samples), v350ms: positive int (samples), v1200ms: positive int (samples)) -> tuple[list of int (threshold-crossing sample indices), list of int (Y-max within 300 ms of each QRS)]
declaration.docstring: Iterate sample-by-sample through Y, maintaining the combined adaptive threshold MFR = M + F + R. After each QRS event: refresh MM and M within 200 ms, decay M from 200–1200 ms via the slope vector, hold/decay R around the expected RR interval. pdg_node.statement: (Rpeak_indices: list of int, signal: 1-D float array (original ECG), v100ms: positive int (samples)) -> 1-D int array of R-peak sample indices
pdg_node.informal_desc: For each candidate R-peak index from the energy domain, search a ±100 ms window on the original ECG signal to find the true amplitude maximum, deduplicate and sort the result, and return a clean integer array of R-peak sample indices.

declaration.name: refine_rpeaks_on_raw_signal
declaration.type_signature: (Rpeak_indices: list of int, signal: 1-D float array (original ECG), v100ms: positive int (samples)) -> 1-D int array of R-peak sample indices
